title: Failed vs Successful Login Correlation
story: Multiple failed login attempts have been detected across several user accounts.
The pattern suggests a credential stuffing attack — trying leaked passwords
against multiple accounts from a single source.
One account was compromised after repeated failures. Investigate the full
timeline: which account, what happened after compromise, and what data was accessed.

objectives.label: How many failed attempts preceded the successful login?
Is the attacker IP internal or external?

hints: Start with: index=hex_lab sourcetype=hex_case2_stream_http http_method=POST uri="/login"
Use: ... | stats count by src_ip | sort -count
Look for the pattern: many failures from one IP, then a success
Check post-compromise activity: index=hex_lab sourcetype=hex_case2_stream_http src_ip=10.0.0.55
splunk_queries: index=hex_lab sourcetype=hex_case2_stream_http http_method=POST uri="/login" | stats count by src_ip, status | sort -count
index=hex_lab sourcetype=hex_case2_stream_http src_ip=10.0.0.55 | table _time uri status
